headers.X-Payment-Required: x402; network=solana-mainnet; asset=usdc; amount=0.08; recipient=<provider_pubkey>; nonce=<server_nonce>

body.narrative: BaaS rails quote returned for the requested neobank corridor. The provider operates as a sponsor-bank intermediary with direct access to ACH, FedWire, RTP, FedNow, SEPA, and Pix rails. For a USDC-native neobank, the typical pattern is: customer initiates an off-ramp from their USDC wallet, the orchestration layer mints a sponsored bank instruction against the rail with the lowest settlement cost that meets the customer's speed requirement, and the provider returns a signed acknowledgment that gets persisted as a memo against the originating Solana transaction. Pix in particular is interesting for LATAM neobanks: 24/7 settlement, sub-10-second finality, and operator fees a fraction of ACH. The trade-off is that Pix counterparty risk is concentrated at the Brazilian Central Bank's settlement layer, so reserves held on rail must be sized to the largest expected per-day outflow. For US neobanks targeting payroll use cases, RTP+FedNow combined coverage now exceeds 70% of receiving deposit institutions, making it viable as a primary rail with ACH only as a fallback for edge banks. Compliance: every rail call here requires the customer's KYC artifact (per the kyc-provider-stub skill) and an OFAC re-screen at the time of instruction submission.
